channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: On social media, Venezuelans post rescues and anguish
item.description: Venezuela is reeling from two massive earthquakes Wednesday night.
item.content:encoded: Venezuela is reeling from two massive earthquakes Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Mia Venkat

item.title: A state of emergency is declared in Venezuela as quakes' death toll is expected to rise
item.description: Venezuela is reeling after twin record-breaking earthquakes kill hundreds and flatten buildings, with the death toll expected to rise.
item.content:encoded: Venezuela is reeling after twin record-breaking earthquakes kill hundreds and flatten buildings, with the death toll expected to rise.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: Supreme Court rules that Trump has virtually unchecked power to end TPS program
item.description: The Supreme Court gave the Trump administration the go-ahead to begin mass deportations of hundreds of thousands of Haitians who have been living and working legally in the U.S. for years.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court gave the Trump administration the go-ahead to begin mass deportations of hundreds of thousands of Haitians who have been living and working legally in the U.S. for years.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Supreme Court gives Monsanto a win in its fight against liability from weed killer
item.description: The Supreme Court handed a victory to the maker of the popular weed killer Roundup and made it harder for people to sue over potentially harmful pesticides.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court handed a victory to the maker of the popular weed killer Roundup and made it harder for people to sue over potentially harmful pesticides.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: In Gaza, nowhere is safe despite ceasefire
item.description: As the world focused on the war with Iran, Israeli forces steadily took more territory in the Gaza Strip and killed more people last month than at any time this year, according to Gaza health records.
item.content:encoded: As the world focused on the war with Iran, Israeli forces steadily took more territory in the Gaza Strip and killed more people last month than at any time this year, according to Gaza health records.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: What made the deadly Venezuelan earthquakes different
item.description: It appears the two big earthquakes in Venezuela that occurred in rapid succession may have involved two separate fault lines. Several faults intersect in this tectonically complex region.
item.content:encoded:
It appears the two big earthquakes in Venezuela that occurred in rapid succession may have involved two separate fault lines. Several faults intersect in this tectonically complex region.

(Image credit: Edilzon Gamez)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Trump works to woo farmers in aftermath of Iran war
item.description: President Trump's proposal to have Iran use their unfrozen funds to buy American agriculture products says a lot about where he's worried about lost political support.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's proposal to have Iran use their unfrozen funds to buy American agriculture products says a lot about where he's worried about lost political support.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: The new book 'Tropesick' is a love letter to romance novels
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Lauren Okie, whose new book finds two childhood neighbors reunited to ghostwrite a love story for a withdrawn author at her Hampton's estate.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Lauren Okie, whose new book finds two childhood neighbors reunited to ghostwrite a love story for a withdrawn author at her Hampton's estate.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: The screwworm parasite continues to spread in Texas, threatening cattle and wildlife
item.description: The New World screwworm continues to spread across Texas and threatens the cattle industry. Fighting the spread depends on the cooperation of ranchers, but they are suspicious of the government.
item.content:encoded: The New World screwworm continues to spread across Texas and threatens the cattle industry. Fighting the spread depends on the cooperation of ranchers, but they are suspicious of the government.
item.dc:creator: David Martin Davies

item.title: How Coach Mauricio Pochettino made believers out of the U.S. World Cup team
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino was the biggest name the U.S. men's soccer team had ever hired — then, fans and analysts asked whether he'd been worth the hype. Now, the answer is a resounding yes.
item.content:encoded: Mauricio Pochettino was the biggest name the U.S. men's soccer team had ever hired — then, fans and analysts asked whether he'd been worth the hype. Now, the answer is a resounding yes.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan
